session.user:
⏺ Plan saved as PR #9297. Here are your next steps:
Implement PR #9297: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9297 && erk implement (dangerously): erk br co --for-plan 9297 && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9297 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9297 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout PR #9297: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9297 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9297
Dispatch PR #9297: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 9297 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
✻ Sautéed for 1m 36s
we need new suggested commands given for the "slots" case. should be using erk slot co

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 773 chars]...related code.
Be thorough - check both Python source code and Claude skill/command files.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 353 chars]...)
Also check if there's an existing `erk slot` command group and what subcommands it has.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive summary.
## Summary: Next Steps / Suggested Commands Generation
I found the source code that generates the "next steps" / suggested commands displayed after a plan is saved as a PR. Here's the complete breakdown:
### Single Source of Truth
**Primary Location:** - `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/output/next_steps.py`
This module contains:
1. **`PrNextSteps` dataclass** (frozen) - Encapsulates all PR-related commands as properties - `pr_number: int` - The PR/plan number - `url: str` - The PR URL
2. **Property methods** that generate individual commands: - `checkout` → `erk br co --for-plan {pr_number}` - `checkout_new_slot` → `erk br co --new-slot --for-plan {pr_number}` - `implement_current_wt` → `erk br co --for-plan {pr_number} && erk implement` - `implement_current_wt_dangerous` → `erk br co --for-plan {pr_number} && erk implement -d` - `implement_new_wt` → `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan {pr_number} --script)" && erk implement` - `implement_new_wt_dangerous` → `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan {pr_number} --script)" && erk implement -d` - `dispatch` → `erk pr dispatch {pr_number}` - `dispatch_slash_command` → `/erk:pr-dispatch` (static) - `view` → Returns the URL
3. **Formatting functions**: - `format_pr_next_steps_plain(pr_number, *, url)` - Text format with hierarchical display
... [60 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Let me read the key files to plan the changes.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Plan next steps formatting - single source of truth.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass 4→ 5→ 6→@dataclass(frozen=True) 7→class PrNextSteps: 8→ """Canonical commands for plan operations.""" 9→ 10→ pr_number: int 11→ url: str 12→ 13→ @property 14→ def view(self) -> str: 15→ return self.url 16→ 17→ @property 18→ def dispatch(self) -> str: 19→ return f"erk pr dispatch {self.pr_number}" 20→ 21→ @property 22→ def checkout(self) -> str: 23→ return f"erk br co --for-plan {self.pr_number}" 24→ 25→ @property 26→ def checkout_new_slot(self) -> str: 27→ return f"erk br co --new-slot --for-plan {self.pr_number}" 28→ 29→ @property 30→ def dispatch_slash_command(self) -> str:
... [67 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Tests for next_steps formatting.""" 2→ 3→from erk_shared.output.next_steps import ( 4→ PrNextSteps, 5→ format_pr_next_steps_plain, 6→) 7→ 8→ 9→def test_plan_next_steps_constructs_with_all_params() -> None: 10→ """PrNextSteps constructs correctly with plan_number and url.""" 11→ s = PrNextSteps(pr_number=42, url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42") 12→ assert s.pr_number == 42 13→ assert s.url == "https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42" 14→ 15→ 16→def test_plan_next_steps_view_returns_url() -> None: 17→ """view returns the URL directly.""" 18→ s = PrNextSteps(pr_number=42, url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42") 19→ assert s.view == "https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42" 20→ 21→ 22→def test_plan_next_steps_checkout_uses_plan_number() -> None: 23→ """checkout uses --for-plan with plan_number.""" 24→ s = PrNextSteps(pr_number=42, url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42") 25→ assert s.checkout == "erk br co --for-plan 42" 26→ 27→ 28→def test_plan_next_steps_dispatch_uses_plan_number() -> None: 29→ """dispatch uses plan_number.""" 30→ s = PrNextSteps(pr_number=42, url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42")
... [72 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Tests for next_steps formatting functions.""" 2→ 3→from erk_shared.output.next_steps import ( 4→ PrNextSteps, 5→ format_pr_next_steps_plain, 6→) 7→ 8→ 9→class TestPrNextSteps: 10→ def test_construction(self) -> None: 11→ steps = PrNextSteps( 12→ pr_number=42, 13→ url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42", 14→ ) 15→ assert steps.pr_number == 42 16→ 17→ def test_view_returns_url(self) -> None: 18→ steps = PrNextSteps( 19→ pr_number=42, 20→ url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42", 21→ ) 22→ assert steps.view == "https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42" 23→ 24→ def test_dispatch_uses_pr_number(self) -> None: 25→ steps = PrNextSteps( 26→ pr_number=42, 27→ url="https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42", 28→ ) 29→ assert steps.dispatch == "erk pr dispatch 42" 30→

session.assistant: `erk slot co` takes a `BRANCH` argument but doesn't have `--for-plan`. The current next steps all use `erk br co --for-plan {pr_number}` which resolves a plan PR to a branch name.

tool_use.param: [{'question': 'erk slot co takes a BRANCH name, not --for-plan. Should we add --for-plan s...[truncated 528 chars]...nds; erk br co --for-plan should delegate to slot co internally'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

19→def create_plan_saved_marker(session_id: str, repo_root: Path, pr_number: int) -> None: 20→ """Create marker file to indicate plan was saved to GitHub. 21→ 22→ The plan number is stored on the first line so the hook can read it 23→ when building the Step 2 "what next?" prompt. 24→ 25→ Args: 26→ session_id: The session ID for the scratch directory. 27→ repo_root: The repository root path. 28→ pr_number: The plan PR number. 29→ """ 30→ marker_dir = get_scratch_dir(session_id, repo_root=repo_root) 31→ marker_file = marker_dir / "exit-plan-mode-hook.plan-saved.marker" 32→ marker_file.write_text( 33→ f"{pr_number}
" 34→ "Created by: /erk:plan-save
" 35→ "Trigger: Plan was successfully saved to GitHub
" 36→ "Effect: Next ExitPlanMode call will be BLOCKED with Step 2 prompt
" 37→ "Lifecycle: Deleted after being read by next hook invocation
", 38→ encoding="utf-8", 39→ ) 40→ 41→ 42→def read_plan_saved_marker(session_id: str, repo_root: Path) -> int | None: 43→ """Read plan number from the plan-saved marker. 44→ 45→ Args: 46→ session_id: The session ID for the scratch directory. 47→ repo_root: The repository root path. 48→
... [30 lines omitted] ...

133→def create_plan_saved_branch_marker(session_id: str, repo_root: Path, branch_name: str) -> None: 134→ """Create marker file storing the branch name of the saved plan. 135→ 136→ This enables the skipped_duplicate response to include the branch name 137→ when a session tries to save a plan that was already saved. 138→ 139→ Args: 140→ session_id: The session ID for the scratch directory. 141→ repo_root: The repository root path. 142→ branch_name: The branch name where the plan was saved. 143→ """ 144→ marker_dir = get_scratch_dir(session_id, repo_root=repo_root) 145→ marker_file = marker_dir / "plan-saved-branch.marker" 146→ marker_file.write_text(branch_name, encoding="utf-8") 147→ 148→ 149→def get_existing_saved_branch(session_id: str, repo_root: Path) -> str | None: 150→ """Check if this session already saved a plan and return the branch name. 151→ 152→ Args: 153→ session_id: The session ID for the scratch directory. 154→ repo_root: The repository root path. 155→ 156→ Returns: 157→ The branch name if plan was already saved, None otherwise. 158→ """ 159→ marker_dir = get_scratch_dir(session_id, repo_root=repo_root) 160→ marker_file = marker_dir / "plan-saved-branch.marker" 161→ if not marker_file.exists(): 162→ return None
... [10 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/logical-hugging-penguin.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Use `erk slot co` in next-steps output Current context: (wt:erk) (br:plnd/add-slot-checkout-nav-03-20-1715) (pr:#9294)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:plnd/add-"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id a25365e5-2973-4a22-a5df-b9ea3ffe7892 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch. Read the plan from: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/logical-hugging-penguin.md Implement changes, run CI, and optionally 'erk pr submit' when done.
... [6 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
# Plan: Use `erk slot co` in next-steps output
## Context
After saving a plan as a PR, the "next steps" output suggests commands using `erk br co --for-plan <number>`. Now that the slot system is the primary checkout mechanism, these should use `erk slot co <branch_name>` instead, which is more direct (no plan-number resolution needed) and aligns with the slots workflow.
## Changes
### 1. Add `branch_name` to `PrNextSteps` and add slot properties
**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/output/next_steps.py`
- Add `branch_name: str` field to `PrNextSteps` dataclass - Replace the `erk br co` properties with `erk slot co` equivalents: - `checkout` → `erk slot co {branch_name}` - `checkout_new_slot` → `erk slot co {branch_name} --new-slot` - `implement_current_wt` → `erk slot co {branch_name} && erk implement` - `implement_current_wt_dangerous` → `erk slot co {branch_name} && erk implement -d` - `implement_new_wt` → `source "$(erk slot co {branch_name} --new-slot --script)" && erk implement` - `implement_new_wt_dangerous` → same with `-d` - Update `format_pr_next_steps_plain` signature to accept `branch_name` - Update `format_next_steps_markdown` signature to accept `branch_name`
### 2. Update call sites to pass `branch_name`
**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py` (line 341) - `branch_name` is already in scope — pass it through
**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/create_cmd.py` (line 137) - `result.branch_name` is already available — pass it through
**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/exit_plan_mode_hook.py` - Add `plan_saved_branch_name: str | None` to `HookInput` dataclass - In `gather_hook_input()`, when `plan_saved_marker_exists`, also read branch via `get_existing_saved_branch(session_id, repo_root)` - Add `branch_name` param to `build_step2_message()` and pass it through - Update the call at line 513
### 3. Update tests
- `packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/output/test_next_steps.py` - `tests/unit/shared/test_next_steps.py` - Exit plan mode hook tests
## Verification
- Run relevant test files - Grep for remaining `erk br co` references in next_steps.py

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Plan: Use `erk slot co` in next-steps output\nCurrent context: (wt:erk) (...[truncated 417 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
